text_excerpt:
INDICATION:  ___ year old man with left HD line moved// confirm placement

TECHNIQUE:  Chest AP view

COMPARISON:  ___

IMPRESSION: 

Lungs are low volume with worsening pulmonary edema.  Bilateral effusions
right greater than left are unchanged.  There are old healed left-sided rib
fractures.  The ETT, NG tube left IJ line and right IJ line are unchanged. 
Cardiomediastinal silhouette is stable.  No pneumothorax is seen.

EXAMINATION:  LIVER OR GALLBLADDER US (SINGLE ORGAN)

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with known liver failure, question of IVC clot on
previously RUQUS// ?IVC clot

TECHNIQUE:  Grey scale and color Doppler ultrasound images of the abdomen were
obtained.

COMPARISON:  Abdominal ultrasound from ___ and the CT report from ___

FINDINGS: 

LIVER: The hepatic parenchyma appears coarsened. The contour of the liver is
nodular, consistent with cirrhosis. There is no new focal liver mass.

A solid left hepatic mass is re-demonstrated measuring 2.6 x 2.6 x 2.3 cm
arising from the anterior left lobe.  This corresponds to the known lesion
previously characterized by a multiphasic CT from ___.

The main portal vein is patent with hepatopetal flow. There is small volume
ascites.  A large right pleural effusion is noted.

The main portal vein is patent with hepatopetal flow.
The TIPS is patent and demonstrates wall-to-wall flow.
Portal vein and intra-TIPS velocities are as follows:
Main portal vein: 53 cm/sec, previously 40 cm/sec
Proximal TIPS: 222 cm/sec, previously 172cm/sec
Mid TIPS: 127 cm/sec, previously 167 cm/sec
Distal TIPS: 202 cm/sec, previously 188 cm/sec

Flow within the left and right portal vein is not well demonstrated. 
Appropriate flow is seen in the hepatic veins and IVC.  Images of the IVC
demonstrate wall-to-wall appropriate flow were well visualized.  Low-level
echoes within some images of the IVC are felt to be artifactual due to
shadowing from the TIPS and reverberati
[... report truncated ...]

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with decreased bowel sounds, some bloody BMs, q2h
lactulose without movement// ?ileus/obstruction

TECHNIQUE:  Portable supine abdominal radiograph was obtained.

COMPARISON:  Chest x-ray dated ___, CT abdomen pelvis dated ___

FINDINGS: 

There are no abnormally dilated loops of large or small bowel.  There is
paucity of air within the small and large bowel without evidence of
obstruction.
Supine assessment limits detection for free air; there is no gross
pneumoperitoneum.
Osseous structures are unremarkable.
There are embolization coils that project over the left upper, right upper,
and mid upper abdomen, as well as a TIPS shunt over the right upper abdomen. 
There are clips in the left upper abdomen.

IMPRESSION: 

No evidence of obstruction.  There is paucity of air within the small large
bowel.

INDICATION:  ___ year old man intubated and sedated transferred from OSH for
management of sepsis// ?ET placement

TECHNIQUE:  AP portable chest radiograph

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

The patient is intubated with the tip of the endotracheal tube projecting over
the midthoracic trachea.  An esophageal probe is present.  The gastric tube
projects over the stomach.  Bilateral central lines are present, the tips
terminating in the mid SVC.

There is a moderate to large right pleural effusion with subjacent
atelectasis.  Mild left basilar atelectasis is new since prior.  No
pneumothorax.  The lung volumes are decreased.  The size of the cardiac
silhouette is within normal limits.  Embolization coils project over the left
upper quadrant.

IMPRESSION: 

The tip of the endotracheal tube projects over the midthoracic trachea.
